content:
# Final Review Planner Positions
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

Verified the amendment's claim by tracing the full code path. When a run completes successfully (engine.rs:5530 sets RunStatus::Completed), reconciliation happens post-dispatch (daemon_loop.rs:1494-1522). If reconciliation fails, the task is marked Failed and its lease released. However, retry_task (task_service.rs:453-482) only moves the task back to Pending — it does not reset the run snapshot. On the next dispatch cycle, read_run_snapshot (daemon_loop.rs:2373-2375) returns RunStatus::Completed, and dispatch_in_worktree immediately rejects it at line 3007-3010 with TaskStateTransitionInvalid. This creates a permanent fail/retry/fail loop where reconciliation can never be re-attempted through the normal retry path. The amendment correctly identifies a real, actionable bug.

## Votes

- `fr-12-4b0289eb`: Accept
  Verified through code tracing: (1) successful run sets RunStatus::Completed in the snapshot (engine.rs:5530), (2) reconciliation failure marks the daemon task as Failed (daemon_loop.rs:1499-1520), (3) retry_task only transitions task status to Pending without touching the run snapshot (task_service.rs:476), (4) next dispatch reads RunStatus::Completed and rejects with TaskStateTransitionInvalid (daemon_loop.rs:3007-3010). This is a real dead-end: any transient reconciliation error (br sync network failure, milestone store I/O error) produces a permanent fail→retry→fail loop with no automated recovery. The comments at lines 1608-1616 and 1755-1762 explicitly promise that retry_task will 're-dispatch reconciliation against the still-Running milestone', but the run snapshot — which gates dispatch — is already Completed and never gets reset. The fix requires either a dedicated reconciliation-only retry path that bypasses the run status check, or resetting the run status so a retry can re-enter the dispatch function and reach reconciliation code.
